stem: For the exponential function f, the value of f(2) is k, where k is a constant. Which of the following equivalent forms of the function f shows the value of k as the coefficient or the base?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. All four forms are equivalent to f(x) = 8·3x, since 24·3x−1 = (24/3)·3x = 8·3x, 72·3x−2 = (72/9)·3x = 8·3x, and 216·3x−3 = (216/27)·3x = 8·3x. Then k = f(2) = 8·32 = 8·9 = 72. In the form f(x) = 72(3)x−2, evaluating at x = 2 makes the exponent 0, so the coefficient 72 itself equals f(2) = k.

Choice A shows f(0) = 8 as the coefficient. Choice B shows f(1) = 24 as the coefficient. Choice D shows f(3) = 216 as the coefficient.
